LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT IPC INTERNATIONAL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS ISSUED PRIOR TO THE HEARING: DEFENDANT IPC INTERNATIONAL CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE: PLAINTIFF FAILED TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT SHOWING THAT IPC BREACHED ANY DUTY THAT MIGHT HAVE BEEN OWED BY IT TO PLAINTIFF. 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PREMISES LIABILITY: PLAINTIFF ADMITS THAT IPC DOES NOT HAVE AN OWNERSHIP INTEREST IN THE PROPERTY. ONE IS NOT SUBJECT TO PREMISES LIABILITY IF THAT PERSON DOES NOT OWN OR POSSESS THE PREMISES AT ISSUE. ALCAZAR V. VECE (1997) 14 CAL. 4TH 1149, 1162. DEFENDANT IPC TO PROVIDE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER ON THE MOTION. DEFENDANT IPC'S ORDER ON OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SIGNED IN OPEN COURT, OVERRULED IN PART & SUSTAINED IN PART. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, REPORTER: LORETTA NAJERA, CSR #5153

RESPONSE TO SEPARATE STATEMENT IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN TH ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY DEFENDANT ABLE BUILDING MAINTENANCE CAPITAL & COUNTIES USA INC DAILY CITY SERRAMONTE CENTER LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS ISSUED PRIOR TO THE HEARING: DEFENDANTS ABLE BUILDING MAINTENANCE COMPANY, CAPITAL & COUNTIES USA. INC. AND DALY CITY SERRAMONTE CENTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION. DENY MSJ/MSA ON GROUND THAT DEFENDANTS LACKED ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE AND/OR DID NOT BREACH THEIR DUTY. TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REMAIN AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANTS KNEW OR SHOULD HAVE KNOWN THAT THERE WAS LIQUID ON THE FLOOR, AND WHETHER THEY BREACHED THEIR DUTY TO PLAINTIFF BY NOT INSPECTING THE FLOOR WITHIN A REASONABLE TIME. SEE PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO UMF 5, 6, 7, 8. GRANT MSA ON 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION, COUNT TWO (PREMISES LIABILITY; WILLFUL FAILURE TO WARN). PLAINTIFF HAS COME FORWARD WITH NO EVIDENCE WHATSOEVER TO ESTABLISH ANY EXCEPTIONS TO CC SECTION 846 APPLY. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS ORDER ON OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. DEFENDANTS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, REPORTER: LORETTA NAJERA, CSR #5153

Dntc Of Motion To Bifurcate Trial
Off Calendar May-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT IPC INTERNATIONAL CORPORATION'S MOTION TO BIFURCATE TRIAL SO THAT LIABILITY ISSUES ARE TRIED BEFORE DAMAGES ISSUES.

APPEARANCES: Anna Dubrovsky, Esq. & Pavel Krepkiy, Esq. of Anna Dubrovsky Law Group, Inc. 746-1477 for pltf & opposing party; Scott A. Freedman, Esq. of Morris Polich & Purdy LLP 213/417-5317 for deft IPC International Corporation; Ash R. Mohindru, Esq. of Simoncini & Associates 949/677-9682 for defts Able Building Maintenance, Capital & Counties USA Inc. And Daly City Serramonte Center LLC.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling as issued prior to the hearing: Motion to Bifurcate - Deny without prejudice. The motion may be renoticed before the trial judge. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT IPC INTERNATIONAL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Anna Dubrovsky, Esq. & Pavel Krepkiy, Esq. of Anna Dubrovsky Law Group, Inc. 746-1477 for pltf & opposing party; Scott A. Freedman, Esq. of Morris Polich & Purdy LLP 213/417-5317 for deft IPC International Corporation; Ash R. Mohindru, Esq. of Simoncini & Associates 949/677-9682 for defts Able Building Maintenance, Capital & Counties USA Inc. And Daly City Serramonte Center LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as issued prior to the hearing: Defendant IPC International Corp.'s Motion for Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication. Grant motion for summary judgment. 1st cause of action for negligence: Plaintiff failed to raise a triable issue of fact showing that IPC breached any duty that might have been owed by it to plaintiff. 2nd cause of action for premises liability: Plaintiff admits that IPC does not have an ownership interest in the property. One is not subject to premises liability if that person does not own or possess the premises at issue. Alcazar v. Vece (1997) 14 Cal. 4th 1149, 1162. Defendant IPC to provide a proposed form of order on the motion. Defendant IPC's order on objections to evidence signed in open court, overruled in part & sustained in part. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS ABLE BUILDING MAINTENANCE, CAPITAL & COUNTIES USA INC. AND DALY CITY SERRAMONTE CENTER LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Anna Dubrovsky, Esq. & Pavel Krepkiy, Esq. of Anna Dubrovsky Law Group, Inc. 746-1477 for pltf & opposing party; Scott A. Freedman, Esq. of Morris Polich & Purdy LLP 213/417-5317 for deft IPC International Corporation; Ash R. Mohindru, Esq. of Simoncini & Associates 949/677-9682 for defts Able Building Maintenance, Capital & Counties USA Inc. And Daly City Serramonte Center LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as issued prior to the hearing: Defendants Able Building Maintennce Company, Capital & Counties USA. Inc. and Daly City Serramonte Center's Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication. Deny MSJ/MSA on ground that defendants lacked actual or constructive notice and/or did not breach their duty. Triable issues of fact remain as to whether defendants knew or should have known that there was liquid on the floor, and whether they breached their duty to plaintiff by not inspecting the floor within a reasonable time. See Plaintiff's Responses to UMF 5, 6, 7, 8. Grant MSA on 2nd cause of action, Count Two (premises liability; willful failure to warn). Plaintiff has come forward with no evidence whatsoever to establish any exceptions to CC section 846 apply. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Defendants order on objections to evidence signed in open court. Defendants to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153

Joinder In Ipc International Corporations Motion To Bifurcate
Off Calendar May-06-2011 Text Ruling

Mohindru, Esq. of Simoncini & Associates 949/677-9682 for defts Able Building Maintenance, Capital & Counties USA Inc. And Daly City Serramonte Center LLC.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling as issued prior to the hearing: Motion to Bifurcate - Deny without prejudice. The motion may be renoticed before the trial judge. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153
